title: Read-Only Root Filesystem
description:
## Read-Only Root Filesystem

Setting `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true` prevents any process inside the container from writing to the container's root filesystem layer, significantly reducing the blast radius of a compromised container.

**Your task:**

Create a Pod named `readonly-pod` using `busybox:1.36` (command: `sleep 3600`) with:
- `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true` in the container's `securityContext`
- An `emptyDir` volume mounted at `/tmp` to provide writable scratch space

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get pod readonly-pod -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem}'
```

hints.title: readOnlyRootFilesystem with emptyDir
hints.body: Set `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true` in the container's `securityContext`. Mount an `emptyDir` volume at `/tmp` so the process has a writable location if needed.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: readonly-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
    securityContext:
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    volumeMounts:
    - name: tmp-vol
      mountPath: /tmp
  volumes:
  - name: tmp-vol
    emptyDir: {}
EOF
